channel.title: World
channel.description: The Washington Post World section provides information and analysis of breaking world news stories. In addition to our world news and video, Post World News offers discussions and blogs on major international news and economic issues.

item.title: Patriot missile deal and Trump’s praise for Zelensky put pressure on Russia
item.dc:creator: Mary Ilyushina
item.description: The Kremlin downplayed President Donald Trump’s reversal but said it could cause the war to go on longer.

item.title: Trump says U.S. and Iran will keep talking but declares ceasefire ‘OVER!’
item.dc:creator: Rachel Chason
item.description: The president suggested talks could continue amid military strikes, but analysts say a deeply flawed preliminary truce deal poses obstacles for negotiators.

item.title: At least 11 people killed as wildfires sweep southern Spain
item.dc:creator: Anthony Faiola
item.description: The deaths in Andalusia included foreign nationals, and high temperatures raised fears of more fires across Europe.

item.title: U.S. intensifies strikes on Iran’s coast along Strait of Hormuz
item.dc:creator: Rachel Chason, Suzan Haidamous
item.description: After President Donald Trump declared the collapse of a preliminary peace agreement on Wednesday, the U.S. and Iran traded renewed airstrikes.

item.title: Health workers fighting Ebola go on strike after months without pay
item.dc:creator: Rael Ombuor, Rachel Chason
item.description: Doctors and Nurses on the front lines of the Democratic Republic of Congo’s Ebola response have reached a breaking point.

item.title: U.S., Iran trade dozens more strikes in dangerous cycle
item.dc:creator: Michael Birnbaum, Victoria Craw, Dan Lamothe
item.description: For a second straight day, the U.S. military attacked Iranian targets overnight and Iran struck back, targeting U.S. bases in Bahrain and Kuwait.
